STATUS_2026_05_14: DEPRECATED — DO NOT TRUST THIS FILTER OR ITS BACKTEST. See untrustworthy_backtest_note below before recommending V_MULTI, retraining it, or citing its numbers.

untrustworthy_backtest_note.headline: The V_MULTI backtest, walk-forward, and out-of-time numbers are misleading because the 'human_score' used to gate them is NOT the same as the live human algorithm's score.
untrustworthy_backtest_note.what_we_observed_live: On 2026-05-14 we directly compared, in production logs, the live human algorithm's composite_score at the moment it fired an alert (alert_manager.py) vs the V_MULTI filter's reconstructed composite_score at the bot's 1pt-zone entry seconds later (bot_filter.py). The two scores diverged systematically: live=5 IBH-down -> reconstructed=-1; live=8 FIB_EXT_HI up -> reconstructed=4; live=7 -> reconstructed=4; live=7 -> reconstructed=0-2. Gap of 3-9 points.
untrustworthy_backtest_note.why_they_diverge: Both sides call scoring.composite_score (same function), but feed different inputs: (a) live uses LevelState.entry_count (cumulative 7pt-zone entries maintained as in-memory state) and live alert-outcome streaks; (b) reconstruction uses test_count_7pt derived by walking back through ticks plus resolution-order streaks from prior bot trades. These approximations are stale/incorrect vs live, mostly biasing the reconstructed score DOWN.
untrustworthy_backtest_note.implication_for_backtests: Every V_MULTI evaluation (out_of_time_test.py, clean_report.py, vote_aggregation_sweep.py, speed_filter_validate.py, speed_interaction_test.py, bidirectional_test.py) gated training and test on reconstructed_score >= 5. That universe is a SUBSET of the live-alert->=5 universe and shifted in distribution. The +$26.73/day OOT lift and +$9.98/day walk-forward lift describe behavior within the reconstruction-gated universe — not what the bot would do under a live-alert gate. Do not assume those numbers transfer.
untrustworthy_backtest_note.implication_for_models: The 3 LGBMs were trained on reconstruction-gated events. If the gate is switched to live-alert-fired events, the models see a different feature distribution than they trained on and may not generalize. They must be retrained on the live-alert-matched universe before any new lift can be claimed.
untrustworthy_backtest_note.live_outcome_so_far: Over 2026-05-12 through 2026-05-14, V_MULTI took 0 trades while the live human algorithm fired 21 alerts on bot-whitelisted levels (mostly winners). The bot's 1pt zones DID open on those levels at the right times — V_MULTI rejected them because the reconstructed score under-counted the live score.
untrustworthy_backtest_note.decision: Switch the bot to gate on live human alerts (alert_manager.check_and_notify fired list) instead of reconstructed score + LGBM votes. V_MULTI stays in the repo as code but is OFF (BOT_FILTER_ENABLED=False). When we build the next iteration, training data must be matched to live alerts (not offline reconstruction), and the validation pipeline must read from the alerts DB, not from add_human_score.py.

filter_description: Secondary gate on top of bot's existing trigger logic. When BOT_FILTER_ENABLED=True in config.py, a trade is taken only if (a) human composite_score >= 5 at the 7pt-zone-entry moment, AND (b) all 3 LightGBM models below agree that expected_pnl > 0 at their respective TP/SL geometry. The bot's own per-level TP/SL config (BOT_PER_LEVEL_TS) still controls the actual bracket placed; the models are used purely as quality gates.
dataset: _events_augmented_v4.parquet (62,403 events offline-labeled; trained universe restricts to human_score >= 5 and lunch-suppression off = 10,185 events)

deployment.integration_point: mnq_alerts/bot_trader.py BotTrader._process_zone_entries(), after all existing filters (direction, momentum, caps, hybrid, counter-trend, stale-approach, vol, bot_entry_score) and before broker.can_trade()
deployment.reversibility: Set BOT_FILTER_ENABLED=False and restart the bot. The filter call is wrapped in a conditional; when False, NO state is maintained (tick buffer, per-direction counter, etc. all dormant) and behavior is byte-for-byte identical to pre-filter bot.
deployment.tick_buffer_size: rolling 15 minutes; trimmed on every tick
deployment.decision_latency_ms: estimated 3-5ms per decision (feature compute + 3 LGBM predicts). Negligible vs IBKR's 50-200ms order round-trip.
deployment.fail_open: If feature compute or model predict raises an exception, the filter returns take=True with reason='error'. This keeps the bot functioning if the filter has a bug; review broker logs for '[bot_filter]' lines to detect.

backtest_results.out_of_time_window: 2026-03-24 to 2026-05-06 (30 trading days, 955 candidate events never seen in training)
backtest_results.caveat: Baseline in this dataset is negative because the reconstructed human_score uses approximated test_count (1pt-zone counts vs 7pt-zone counts in live) and resolution-order leak-safe streak vs live's sequential streak. Live human alerts in the same period had ~84.7% WR (positive P&L). The FILTER'S +$26.73/day improvement should compound onto whatever the live human-style base actually produces.
backtest_results.STATUS_2026_05_14_UPDATE: Live evidence (2026-05-12 to 2026-05-14) shows the reconstructed_score is materially LOWER than the live human score on the same events, so the +$26.73/day number does NOT describe what V_MULTI does under a live-alert gate. See untrustworthy_backtest_note at top of file.

walk_forward_results.folds: Q2'25, Q3'25, Q4'25, Q1'26 (rolling 90-day train window, 1-day embargo)

feature_set: 51 features: 30 v1 (kinematics, aggressor, volume, level context, vol/time) + 11 zone (7-to-1pt approach window) + 10 v2 (jerk, round-number proximity, large prints, session range, time-since-extremes, pre-zone volume, approach consistency, relative volume) + 7 one-hot level identifiers. See feature_list.json for canonical order.
retraining: Run mnq_alerts/scoring_rework/train_production_models.py to retrain from the latest _events_augmented_v4.parquet. The augment pipeline (augment_dataset.py -> add_features.py -> merge_multi_labels.py -> add_human_score.py) regenerates the parquet from scratch in ~3 minutes.
monitoring: Each filter decision logs '[bot_filter] TAKE|SKIP <level> | reason=<r> score=<s> votes=<v>/3 ...'. Aggregate these to track filter agreement vs base bot over time. Flag if model_probs systematically drift toward 1.0 (calibration issue) or take_rate drops below ~20% (filter too aggressive).
